statement: Null `samples.score_id` and `samples.score_critic_id` are expected on modern subset-scoring runs because score completion is stored on `sample_evidence_scores` and the `scores` / `score_critics` tables.
rationale: Code paths, schema semantics, and a clean D1 run all show sample-level score pointers remain null while unit-level score artifacts are fully complete.
keyAssumptions: The runs under review use the modern subset-scoring path with `sample_evidence_scores` materialized.
validationCriteria: Clean runs show unit-level score completion with null sample-level score pointers.
Score-stage apply paths patch unit rows rather than sample rows when score units exist.
critiquePoints: Historical legacy runs without score units may still rely on sample-level score pointers.
questions: Do any production reports or exports still read sample-level score pointers as authoritative on modern runs?
